meta.description: Forbidden patterns and allowed context rules for audit template instantiation

forbidden_patterns.name: condition-cell-overflow
forbidden_patterns.description: Declaring or using ConditionCell<BITS> with BITS > 8, violating the 'Need 9 means split' law. The Blue River Dam covenant limits condition cells to 8 bits.

forbidden_patterns.name: raw-evidence-exported-as-admitted
forbidden_patterns.description: Exporting or returning Evidence<T, Raw, W> as if it were Admitted, or using a Raw-state evidence where Admitted is required. This violates the one-way-door law.

forbidden_patterns.name: silent-structure-loss
forbidden_patterns.description: Performing a lossy transformation (e.g., OCEL → XES, removing object links) without emitting a LossReport. The loss is hidden from audit.

forbidden_patterns.name: soundness-forged
forbidden_patterns.description: Constructing or manipulating a WfNetConst<SoundnessWitnessed> without passing through wasm4pm's soundness verification. Soundness witnesses are non-forgeable.

forbidden_patterns.name: witness-discrimination-violation
forbidden_patterns.description: Using Admission<T, W1> in place of Admission<T, W2>, or merging evidence admitted against different witnesses without proper re-admission. Witness tags are sealed.

allowed_contexts.name: internal-bridge-assembly
allowed_contexts.exception_reason: The Wasm4pmBridge and InternalBridge witness markers are used in src/witness.rs to tag internal bridge code. Some forbidden patterns are allowed during bridge assembly for compatibility reasons.

allowed_contexts.name: named-projection
allowed_contexts.exception_reason: The src/loss.rs module explicitly implements loss accounting. Lossy transformations are allowed here only if they use the Project trait and emit a LossReport. This is not a blanket exception — loss must be named and recorded.

allowed_contexts.name: wasm4pm-graduation
allowed_contexts.exception_reason: The src/graduation.rs module and the wasm4pm feature gate are entry points to the wasm4pm engine. Some otherwise forbidden patterns (e.g., non-forgeable witness construction) are allowed here because the wasm4pm engine is a trusted execution boundary.
